regime: Trend Down, Fresh Flip (Oil Shock Risk-Off)
summary: Risk-off, and it came from oil. Middle East escalation sent WTI up 6.82% to 92.39 overnight, and that inflation catalyst landed on top of a jobless claims print of 187,000, the lowest since 1969. Hot labor plus an oil shock pushed yields and the dollar up, and equities and gold both got sold. MNQ closed -1.47%, MES -1.13%, MGC -2.00%, MCL +6.82%. The structural part matters most: NQ, ES, and gold all closed below their 1hr ATR trailing stops and flipped short, the first synchronized equity-and-gold trend flip in weeks. Crude flipped the other way, long. NQ dumped 380 points to a 28,432.50 low, then bounced almost 300 points to close 28,740.75, back above its session AVWAP but still under its stop. Not financial advice.
psychology: A stop flip is information, not an instruction. Three markets you may have been long in flipped short in one session, and the pull now is to spin the whole book short at the lows, right after a 380-point drop, into a close that already bounced 300 points. That is chasing. The signal turning down means the long thesis is off the table until price earns it back, not sell here. The oil trade is the other trap: crude up 6.8% on a headline is the hardest move to chase because the gap already happened. If you were not positioned before the news, the edge is gone and all that is left is risk. Sitting out a move you missed is a skill.

event.name: Initial jobless claims 187,000 (scheduled) plus a Middle East oil supply shock (geopolitical)
event.actual: Jobless claims 187,000, the lowest since 1969, printed 08:30 ET inside the COMPASS window. WTI up 6.82% to 92.39 on Middle East supply risk.
event.surprise: Labor far stronger than expected (hawkish), compounded by an oil supply shock (inflationary). Both pressure equities and gold.
event.nqReaction: Textbook risk-off with an inflation twist. NQ sold from the open, shedding 380 points to 28,432.50 before a late bounce. The 1hr trend flipped short, the first flip since mid-month.
event.crossAsset: The tell is gold. Metal fell 2.00% on a day of geopolitical stress because the driver was higher real yields and a firmer dollar, not a growth scare. Oil up, stocks down, gold down, dollar up is a coherent inflation-shock signature.

observe.name: ES / NQ confirmation
observe.note: The alignment that was weakening yesterday is now confirmed to the downside: both flipped short on the same session. Watch which one reclaims its stop first as the early sign of a pause.

observe.name: Gold / equity correlation
observe.note: Gold sold with stocks today, a real-yields regime rather than a risk-off hedge. Watch whether it decouples if the Middle East premium reasserts over the weekend.

observe.name: Volume / liquidity
observe.note: The chart volume series returned empty via the API this run. Liquidity is inferred elevated on the 369-point range. Flagged to re-measure next run.

risk.ddProximity: Not binding. Book entered flat, full daily budget intact.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Full budget intact, but per-trade size is cut at the DEFENSIVE band.
risk.sizing: This is the environment DEFENSIVE exists for. ATR(14) expanded 38% in a day, three markets flipped trend together, and crude is moving on headlines that gap. Cut size, trade the post-open range only after it settles, and avoid holding oil exposure through a headline. Stops are overhead resistance for equities and gold now, so shorts have a defined risk line while longs have none until a stop reclaims.
risk.note: FOMC July 28 to 29 next week. Friday brings July flash PMIs (09:45 ET) and June New Home Sales (10:00 ET). The binding risk is a weekend gap on Middle East oil headlines. Size overnight and weekend exposure for a Sunday-night gap.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 (short)
setups.entry: Down-signal active below the 28,883.90 MNQ stop
setups.bias: Short with the fresh trend flip

setups.name: AVWAP Hand-off (MNQ, short)
setups.entry: OR down-break handed off through the AVWAP

tomorrowsMap.if: MNQ reclaims the 28,883.90 stop on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: the short flip is voided and the 29,000 psych, then the 29,119 prior-low shelf, come back into play
tomorrowsMap.bias: The stop is the line. Below it the trend is down and rallies are sells. A 2-close reclaim is the only thing that repairs the long

tomorrowsMap.if: MNQ closes below the 28,614.86 AVWAP on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: the 28,500 psych then the 28,432.50 RTH low are the downside tests
tomorrowsMap.bias: Below the AVWAP the sellers keep control. The late bounce closed at +2.05 sigma over the AVWAP, so losing it says the bounce failed

tomorrowsMap.if: MES reclaims the 7,483.81 stop on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: the short flip voids and the 7,500 psych opens back up
tomorrowsMap.bias: ES is the confirmation tell again. It flipped short with NQ, so a reclaim here first would signal the broad de-risk is pausing

tomorrowsMap.if: MGC closes back above the 4,086.59 stop on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: the short flip is voided and the 4,100 psych comes back into scope
tomorrowsMap.bias: Gold lost both its stop and its AVWAP. Below the stop the metal is broken. A close under the 4,042.30 RTH low opens the 4,000 round

tomorrowsMap.if: MCL holds above the 90.88 stop on hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: the long stays live and the 93.50 RTH high, then the 95 psych, are the upside tests
tomorrowsMap.bias: Long above the stop while the trend and the daily change agree. This is headline-driven, so size for a reversal gap. A 2-close break of 90.88 voids the long

yesterdaysMapScore.note: The map was a symmetric reclaim-or-lose structure, and the tape took the downside branch on every row that fired. All three lose-the-level scenarios triggered and all three played out. The two reclaim branches never came into play because nothing bounced. The downside read was accurate.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidenceEvidence
DirectionTREND DOWN, FRESH FLIPHighMNQ, MES, and MGC all closed below their 1hr ATR stops and flipped short. MNQ -1.47%, MES -1.13%, MGC -2.00%, MCL +6.82% (flipped long). First synchronized equity-and-gold flip in weeks.
VolatilityHIGH, EXPANDINGHighMNQ ATR(14) jumped to 137.09 from 99.26 on Jul 22, up 38% in a day. RTH range 369.25 points. RTH sigma 61.42.
SessionTREND-DOWN DRIVE, LATE BOUNCEHighOvernight held near 29,283, RTH sold from the open to a 28,432.50 low, then bounced roughly 300 points into a 28,740.75 close. A down-day with a recovery tail.
InventorySHORT, COVERED LATEMediumSellers drove the range down; the late 300-point bounce is short-covering and dip-buying into the close, resetting inventory off the low extreme.
EventSCHEDULED plus GEOPOLITICAL SHOCKHighJobless claims 187K, lowest since 1969, printed 08:30 ET inside the COMPASS window. A Middle East supply shock drove WTI +6.8%. Both hawkish and inflationary for stocks and gold.
LiquidityELEVATED (inferred)LowThe chart volume series was not retrievable via the API this run, so volume was not measured. A 369-point MNQ range and a broad flip almost always run above-average volume. Flagged inferred, not measured.

fullReport.title: Strategy Habitat Check
fullReport.html:
StrategyHabitatRead
TSMOM-20IN_HABITAT (short)The cleanest fit today. The 1hr trend flipped short across MNQ, MES, and MGC on the same session, and the momentum edge now has a valid down-signal with MNQ 143 points below its 28,883.90 stop.
AVWAP Hand-offIN_HABITAT (short only)Short-only edge, and today had its exact shape: the opening range broke down, then price handed off into a sustained slide through the AVWAP.
AVWAP Deviation BandBORDERLINEMNQ closed at +2.05 sigma over its 28,614.86 AVWAP, right at the +2s band. In a down-trend that is a reversion-short reference. The +2s to +3s zone is the first fade area if the bounce stalls.
AVWAP MidrangeBORDERLINEThe back half of the session ranged between the AVWAP and the OR extremes as price bounced, which is the midrange band. The strong directional first half is not this edge's habitat.
AVWAP Back Into RangeBORDERLINEThe wide 227.75 OR is the environment this edge likes, but the pattern did not fire: today's OR break was DOWN and held, a continuation rather than a failed break that returns inside.
Out of habitatE3v2 Momentum Long, AVWAP BounceE3v2 gates off any long breakout in a fresh down-flip. AVWAP Bounce has no long bounce with price below its stop and AVWAP structure.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked
fullReport.html:
#Row (as published Jul 22)StatusGradeWhat happened
1MNQ reclaims the 29,247.36 AVWAP on 2 hourly closesNOT TRIGGERED-Price never came close. The overnight topped near 29,283 and sold off; the RTH high was 28,801.75, over 400 points under the AVWAP. The reclaim branch was never in play.
2MNQ closes below the 29,119.00 RTH low on 2 hourly closesTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTFired immediately and stayed fired. MNQ closed under 29,119 on every RTH hour, sliced the 29,098.71 -3s and the 29,077.40 stop, and closed 28,740.75. The trend flipped short exactly as the row warned.
3MES closes below the 7,524.16 stop on 2 hourly closesTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTLost the 7,524.16 stop, voided the Jul 21 long flip, and traded through the 7,503.25 overnight low to a 7,411.75 low, closing 7,455.50.
4MGC reclaims the 4,148.74 AVWAP on 2 hourly closesNOT TRIGGERED-The reclaim never set up. Gold sold from the open and closed 4,052.40, down 2.00%. The row's long condition inverted: gold lost the 4,122.27 stop it wanted held and flipped short.
5MCL closes back above the 88.36 stop on 2 hourly closesTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTReclaimed the 88.36 stop overnight, voided the short, cleared the 88.61 retest, and ran to 93.50 before closing 92.39, up 6.82%. The gap-risk caveat was the whole story.
